mapping.a_element: measurement/sparsity ratio governing detectability (m relative to k log(n-k))
sharp recoverable/non-recoverable threshold in the measurement scaling

brief_justification: Beyond the generic 'sharp threshold in a random high-dimensional ensemble' motif, the governing machinery differs: paper A derives its threshold from information-theoretic converse bounds and a maximum-correlation estimator (estimation/SNR analysis, explicitly avoiding sophisticated methods), whereas paper B uses the statistical-mechanics cavity method at zero temperature, computing a spin-glass phase diagram with metastable-state proliferation and deriving a survey-propagation algorithm. There is no role-for-role correspondence between max-correlation estimation and cavity/survey propagation, and A has no analogue of B's RSB intermediate phase, so the transferable mechanism is not shared.
